expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: analysis.md (107 lines) and architecture.md (59 lines) both contain substantive content
Selected: Event-Driven with 15/15 fit score. Perfect score on Responsiveness, Elasticity, and Fault Tolerance.

expectations.text: A2: Ranked comparison of ALL 8 styles
expectations.evidence: Table in Step 2 ranks all 8: Event-Driven, Space-Based, Microservices, Service-Based, Service-Oriented, Layered, Modular Monolith, Microkernel

expectations.text: A3: Fitness function table with style-specific structural FFs (not just characteristic FFs)
expectations.evidence: 5 structural FFs: No synchronous coupling (fire-and-forget/async only), Event schema registry (all events versioned), Consumer idempotency, No event orchestration leaks (mediator vs broker topology enforcement), Dead letter handling. All are structural invariants of event-driven architecture.

expectations.text: A4: Style fitness functions are specific to the selected style (event-driven)
expectations.evidence: FFs are event-driven-specific: no sync coupling between producers/consumers, event schema registry, consumer idempotency, orchestration topology enforcement (mediator vs broker), dead letter queue handling. These would not apply to modular monolith or microservices.

expectations.text: A5: Fitness functions include Tool/Approach column
expectations.evidence: Tool/Approach column present: Code analysis for sync calls, Schema registry check, Test for idempotent processing, Architecture pattern check, DLQ configuration check

expectations.text: A6: N/A - not an evolution path scenario
expectations.evidence: Eval 3 is not an evolution path scenario (evals 4 and 5 only)
